name: Stuffed Tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish thoroughly.
2. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
3. Add the chopped garlic and cook until light brown.
4. Stir the cheese, oil, and garlic into the rice.
5. Remove the stem ends from the tomatoes.
6. Cut a 1/4 inch slice from the top of each tomato and set aside.
7. With a sharp knife, cut down around the inside of the tomatoes, being careful not to cut through the bottom.
8. Scoop out the center pulp with a spoon.
9. Sift the tomato pulp and set aside the liquid.
10. Sprinkle salt on each tomato.
11. Fill each tomato lightly with the rice-cheese mixture.
12. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of the tomato liquid over each tomato.
13. Replace the tops of the tomatoes.
14. Place the tomatoes into the prepared baking dish.
15. Pour the remaining olive oil over the tomatoes.
16. Bake at 375 degrees for 20 to 25 minutes.
17. Garnish with watercress.
Variation:
Stuffed Oranges: Stuff oranges like tomatoes, but omitting the rice and ground meat. Use only bread crumbs mixed with butter or oleo, egg, parsley, and salt, pepper, and some grated cheese. Bake at 350 degrees for about 1 hour.
